methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Vivodyne is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Andrei Georgescu and Dan Huh that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage vicinity—including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), and Neko Health ($1.8B, Series B)—as well as 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression modeling, using the $40M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.0x multiplier for Series A advancement, implies an $80M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across 7 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp P/S anchoring is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, reflecting stage and business-model heterogeneity within early-stage biotech.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $68M to $931M, with a median of $445M. The wide band reflects uncertainty around whether Vivodyne tracks toward lower-maturity comparables or toward better-capitalized peers like Eikon and Xaira. Key sensitivities include clinical progress, capital efficiency relative to peer cohort, and near-term financing trajectory.
